title: Table Management on ODBC
text: Table Management on ODBC Tables are created on the fly, or by SQL scripts generated by the Create Table facility. When you first attempt to access a table, Uniface needs to know if it exists. If the table does not exist, Uniface creates and indexes it, using the information from the application model. Taking into account the value of the key syntax connector option, the table is created as follows: The create table statement is issued. The primary key constraint is created within table creation. Indexes are created with the create index statement. Overflow Tables Overflow tables can be created on the fly. Entities that contain one of the following Uniface packing codes require both a standard table and an overflow table, if the data source does not support SQL_LONGVARCHAR or SQL_LONGVARBINARY: C* VC* U* VU* S* VS* R* VR* An overflow table contains the primary key from the base table, and the overflow information from the field which causes the overflow table. The name for an overflow table consists of the same name as the entity name with the prefix ‘ o ’ (lower case) or ‘ O ’ (upper case), depending on the connector option identifier case (or id case ). Note: The overflow mechanism is a technical encoding for larger amounts of data, and is not transparent to other applications. Related Links Overflow Tables DBMSs may impose upper limits to the number of fields in a table, the length of a field, and the length of a record. Some DBMSs do not support variable-length fields with a high maximum length. To overcome these limitations, Uniface provides an overflow mechanism, in which data that is represented as a single modeled entity is automatically split into two tables—the base table and an overflow table.
